'''Items''' are loose objects dropped by most breakable [[:Category:Building|structures]], [[Enemies|enemies]], and [[Trader|traders]]. Items can be carried around and thrown. Most can be used in the inventory, health panel, or swung.

There are currently '''432''' items (including inaccessible items) in the Itch.io build. <!-- need someone to sort through which ones -->

== Item weight ==

{{Main|Encumbrance}}

[[File:Dressing weight tooltip.png|thumb|The bottom of the tooltip description of [[dressing]]. Notice the weight tag at the top of the block.]]

An item's weight determines the cost it imposes on the player's carrying capacity and the [[Items#Containers|containers]] it can fit in. Weight incentivizes players to make decisions and plan ahead with which items to keep as they encounter more.

Heavier items give more recoil when thrown. Many items lose weight as they lose condition, a trait named '''scale weight''', which linearly goes from full weight at {{SCAV/Condition|100%|label=yes}} to 0.1u at {{SCAV/Condition|0%|label=yes}}.<ref>Item.cs:Item.totalWeight</ref>

== Condition ==

[[File:Dressing condition tooltip.png|thumb|The top of the tooltip description of [[dressing]]. Notice the condition, a colored percentage.]]

An item's condition determines the amount of time or uses it has, in the context of decay rate and condition loss on use respectively. Most items are destroyed when their condition reaches 0%. Many items can have their condition transferred onto other identical items, done by dragging the item onto another item.

The tooltip's condition changes color as it decreases, from {{SCAV/Condition|100%}} to {{SCAV/Condition|50%}}, {{SCAV/Condition|1%}}, and {{SCAV/Condition|0%}}.

Items sometimes give their full effect when used from the inventory, regardless of their condition. This means that, for example, a [[Neural Booster]] at {{SCAV/Condition|1%|label=yes}} and another [[Neural Booster]] at {{SCAV/Condition|15%|label=yes}} will give the same effects when consumed.

* Note that, in general, liquids do scale most of their effects correctly to the actual volume consumed.

{{Anchor|Wetness}}Certain items can become wet upon contact with [[liquids]]. Wet items passively lose condition 6 times faster<ref>Item.cs:Item.HandleDecay()</ref>.
items like the [[shotgun]], [[pistol]], [[makeshift rifle]],  [[rifle]], and [[jetpack]] have special interactions with [[liquids]], as upon getting wet they have a flat +4.5% increased chance of malfunctioning.<ref>GunScript.cs:GunScript.JamChance()</ref> [[Torch|Torches]] and [[Campfire|Campfires]] are immediately set to {{SCAV/Condition|0%|label=yes}} and extinguished upon becoming wet.<ref>CustomItemBehaviour.cs:CustomItemBehaviour.Update()</ref> Items stay wet for 120 seconds.<ref>Item.cs:Item.isWet</ref>

== Crafting ==

{{Main|Crafting}}

Certain items can be combined in the inventory to create new items.

The player can open the crafting menu by pressing C or clicking the button in the bottom left corner of the screen.

Some items have special qualities that make them useful for certain crafting recipes, such as "Hammering" or "Foliage."

{{Spoiler warning}} [[Scav Prototype]] is organized into '''layers''', each with their own collections of [[tiles]], [[buildings|structures]], [[buildings#Hazards|traps]], [[enemies]], and [[items|items]]. There are currently '''5''' accessible layers in the Itch.io demo build and the 6.x Steam demo. ==Description== Each layer is '''307.2m''' tall. The player must go through these layers to progress in the game. There is currently no limit to the amount of layers the player can go through, and the game goes back to [[Deeper_gravel_lands|deeper gravel lands]] after [[overgrown depths]] is completed. Upon generation of each layer, there can rarely be a [[Layer modifiers|Layer modifier]] added. By default, the amount of [[buildings#Hazards|traps]] and [[Items|items]] changes per layer. This can be adjusted in the [[Controls|main menu]]. One block in the world is exactly 0.30 by 0.30 metersAssembly-CSharp.dll:WorldGeneration.cs:WorldGeneration.PlayerLayerDepthMeters() and is 1 Unity unit by 1 Unity unit.SampleScene.unity Gravity in the game is set to 55 Unity units / s2,Physics2DSettings.asset or 16.5 m/s2 (including in the [[Tutorial]]), which is a bit less than twice Earth's gravity. ==Layers in order== {{Clear}}
[[File:Gravel lands_icon.png|18px]]
=== Gravel Lands === [[File:Gravel lands layer list.png|right|300px]] The [[gravel lands]] is a cavernous, cold layer made up of small interconnected caves. Most of the layer is made of light stone, gravel, soil, and clay, with [[groundwater]] pools filling in any gaps. Wooden scaffolding generates around the layer, and are easy to dig through. Climbing Ropes generate occasionally, which can be climbed to reach different sections of the layer. Holes in the background illuminate parts of the layer, providing some lighting. Only [[shade crawler]]s spawn here, and [[buildings#Hazards|traps]] are limited to [[geyser|geysers]], [[jump pad|jump pads]], and [[spike trap|spike traps]]. Falls are usually short. This layer serves as the start for all runs, and will never reappear later in the run. {{Clear}}
[[File:Deeper_gravel lands_icon.png|20px]]
===Deeper Gravel Lands=== [[File:Deeper gravel lands layer list.png|right|300px]] The [[deeper gravel lands]] is a direct continuation of the gravel lands, bearing many of the same characteristics, along with more dangerous hazards. Unlike the gravel lands, there are no illuminating holes in the background, and wooden scaffolding is replaced by concrete beams. [[Lumalgae]] can be found alongside [[groundwater]]. [[Barbed fence|Barbed fences]], [[bear trap|bear traps]], and [[cave tick|cave tick swarms]] appear in this layer, along with the hazards from the gravel lands. Sand and sandstone will appear as the player makes it towards the end of the layer. * The game currently loops from this layer once the [[overgrown depths]] layer is completed, doing so until the player ends the run (whether forcefully or willingly). {{Clear}}
[[File:Dried_desert_icon.png|17px]]
===Dried Desert=== [[File:Dried desert layer list.png|right|300px]] The [[dried desert]] is a desert-themed layer with spacious caves made of sand and hard stones. Tall drops, [[turret|turrets]], [[coil|coils]], [[stalactites]], and [[landmine|landmines]] make mistakes much more punishing compared to the previous layers, and force the player to look in every direction. [[Waste pipe|Waste pipes]] and [[oil]] can be found alongside [[groundwater]], the latter of which is rarer to find in the desert. As oil is more viscous and harder to escape in this layer, deaths caused by drowning via fractures or exhaustion poses a new challenge. However, this layer provides new tools, including very powerful [[Rose light]]s, as well as sand vines that can be climbed, similarly to the climbing ropes that appear in the [[gravel lands]]. Recharge station structures also generate here, though they are frequently flooded with oil. [[Wall biter|Wall biters]] and [[Shade crawler|Shade crawlers]] are [[enemies]] that spawn. The layer tests the player's ability to spot hazards, encouraging a slow and careful approach through tall drops, large caves, and obscured areas. {{Clear}}
[[File:Wasteland_icon.png|18px]]
=== Wasteland === [[File:Wasteland layer list.png|right|300px]] The [[wasteland]] is a radioactive fallout-themed layer sharing many characteristics with the dried desert. While the layer lacks [[enemies]] of any kind, [[sound cannon|sound cannons]], [[tiles|toxirock]], and [[spent fuel]] force the player to decisively route through the layer, whilst spotting and avoiding the dangerous threats from the previous layer. Durable brick blocks can sometimes be found around the layer, and verdure will start appearing as the player makes it towards the end of the layer. {{Clear}}
[[File:Overgrown depths_icon.png|18px]]
===Overgrown Depths=== [[File:Overgrown depths layer list.png|right|300px]] The [[overgrown depths]] also known as The Jungle is a jungle-themed layer filled with thick vegetation. [[Skullcrusher]]s, and [[grabber Plant|grabber plant]]s are new traps found in this area. Luckily, sound cannons and other wasteland traps are not present. Every plant thus far besides cacti will spawn here, and quite frequently, making starvation a non-issue. Because of the abundance of food, drinkable sap and groundwater, and the lack of turrets and landmines, one might mistake this layer for some sort of paradise if it weren't for a very large and very noticeable threat. [[Thornback|Thornbacks]], [[overgrown tick]]s, [[cave tick]]s, and [[elder thornback|elder thornbacks]] are enemies that spawn in this layer as well as wall biters, forming the primary challenge. It is currently the final layer of the game. {{Clear}} ==IN-DEV Layers== These layers are still in development and aren't accessible to the current Itch.io build or the Steam demo. {{Clear}}
[[File:Frozen chasm_icon.png|18px]]
===Frozen Chasm=== The [[frozen chasm]] is an in-development ice-themed layer, not currently accessible in the public Itch.io build. This layer can be accessed if you save upon exiting Layer 5. The main danger planned with the layer is the freezing cold, and to a lesser extent, [[Snow strider]]s and Gunmines, the player must go between [[Ion space heater]]s, [[Xaloris]]es and [[life pod]]s to stay warm and not freeze to death * The layer at the moment will be blank if entered in the current build (v5.1) * The [[frozen chasm]] along with [[Fungal Pools]] have been fully teased within a stream and video (but are still W.I.P) * Normally continuing will loop the player back to the [[Deeper gravel lands]] {{Clear}}
[[File:Fungal pools icon.png|18px]]
===Fungal Pools=== The [[Fungal Pools]] are an in-development layer, continuing after the [[frozen chasm]]. This layer is a fungus themed layer, with many fungus themed blocks, traps and enemies. The main danger within the layer is the constant rain that is very likely to make you freeze to death if sleeping while wet, the player must sleep within [[life pod]]s to avoid this, other dangers include [[sawblade]]s, Side Stabbers and Grabber Shrooms that can easily cause injuries. *The layer isn't able to be loaded in the current build of the game (v7.0). *The [[Fungal Pools]] and [[frozen chasm]] were the 2 layers fully teased within a stream and the Steam trailer (but are still W.I.P). *The layer is the only layer that [[fungali bungali]] can be found and broken to obtain [[bunchunk]] and craft [[procoagulant]]. {{clear}} The layers after 7 will crash when trying to load so do NOT mod your save file to these layers if you don't have a backup to its previous version. === Crystalline Hollow === The Crystalline Hollow is an in-development layer. * This is the same setting as the Crystalline Hollow from Orsoniks' game [https://orsonik.itch.io/gooey Gooey] From what footage and text from the locale has surfaced so far, this layer will revolve around making use of various crystals while avoiding others. The main dangers of the layer are the hectic terrain with very very large drops, the different types of crystals and a flying crystal enemy called [[Manifestation]], its also said that the corpses of said enemy, if not consumed quickly, will turn into 2 Manifestations, though the larger the group of them is, the slower they are. * Crystal types and their effects * Yellow Crystal: acts like [[coil]], but also recharges all batteries on you * Red Crystal blows up * Green Crystal: (radioactive): irradiates you * Green Crystal: (magic): heals you * Purple Crystal: causes [[sepsis]] * Foggy Crystal: heats you up rapidly * Shaky Crystal: throws you (but allows movement, similar to [[bouncecap]]) * Repulsion Crystal: repels you away (has a field that pushes outwards) * Attraction Crystal: pulls you in (has a field that pulls inwards) * Fragile Crystal: shatters when stood on * Teleportation Crystal: links 2 locations, touching it teleports you to the second location and brain flashes you, crystal is see through * Gray Crystal: flashes but doesn't harm you directly, but will drain all your batteries * Bright Crystal: extremely bright, good light source (might blind?) * Hazy Crystal: touching it causes it to blind for a bit, but acts like [[drop capsule]] * Burning Crystal: burns you * Shifting Crystal: switches places (?) (Note: this is info found from the english translation github page by the dev and direct sneak peaks show) (Note: at the moment its not known the exact names of the crystals, im just going off what they do) === Geothermal Vents === The Geothermal Vents are an in-development layer. The main threat here is planned to be extremely high temperatures. Magma has also been shown off in some sneak peeks. === Where We Must Reach === The planned location of the target technology. === The Ascent === Ascent back to the surface. === Surface === The surface of the Target Planet. === Pathway to Freedom === Optional, related to the [[Ending|game endings]]. ==Trivia== * Tracks for the [[frozen chasm]], [[fungal Pools]], [[crystalline Hollow]] and Geothermal Vents can be found in the [[soundtrack]] page. * Reaching the bottom of a layer prompts the player to either save and quit, or continue to the next layer. This disables the player's ability to jump until an option is chosen, so be ready to leave once you reach the bottom. ** The player can use the [[Jetpack]] to get back off of the bottom of the layer, but this does not remove the save/continue prompt from the middle of the screen or return their ability to jump. ** No longer true as of version 5 on itch.io and version 6.x on Steam. * Any [[liquids|liquid]] that touches the bottom of a layer disappears. ** [[Items]] do not, however. This is likely to prevent players from losing the items in their hands permanently due to falling. *It is possible to enter a different layer in the tutorial. ** The Tutorial structure does not despawn, but the traps and enemies in a layer may spawn inside it. ** The Tutorial structure will not spawn if the game is saved and reloaded instead of just continued. {{Disambiguation|Layers|topic=the setting of the game in the lore}} {{Outdated|1=Orsoniks has stated that any information shared through the discord is outdated and may be subject to change, however, lore sourced from in-game will remain accurate.}} == Info ==
Green margins indicate in-game confirmed lore, such as tooltips, comments, notes, and other sources of text within the game.
Orange margins indicate lore sourced from developer notes and comments, usually found in the game's Discord. These are for the most part correct, but may be incomplete or planned.
Red margins indicate implied lore, from both in-game and developer comments, as well as other games. As such, this color may include slight speculation. Take red margins with a grain of salt.
== Your Purpose == {{Quote|Go down... Get the thing if I find it... Okay...|author=Experiment, [[Dialog#Upon_starting_the_game|in one of the lines that they can say after exiting their lifepod]]}}
Players control an [[Experiment]], forced on a mission amongst thousands of other Experiments to obtain a "piece of important experimental technology", which was lost by a "miscalibration error", as a form of behavioral research on the species. Each run begins with a different specimen, with a randomized height, age, and ID at the top of the [[health panel]]. Thousands have been sent before you, [[Corpse|you are not the first]].
The experimental technology, or 'cargo', or 'battery', is a very powerful wireless power device. In Gooey, another game from Orsoniks, the primary reason why every building, lamp, and outlet is powered is due to this device.

[[File:Intro2.png|right|150px]] |-|Line 2= At the center of it was one company. Their progress seemed miraculous, but they guarded their secrets closely. [[File:Intro3.png|right|150px]] |-|Line 3= Years ago they had created a new species: a combination of animal and human DNA they simply called "Experiments." Resistance is not an option. [[File:Intro12.png|right|150px]]
[[Scav Prototype]] takes place in a sci-fi world where humanity progressed into an interstellar, spacefaring society.
Not much is directly said about this society, though it is implied that development and money have taken precedence over ethics.
The original [[Experiment]] (referred to as '''specimen'''), and base of every other Gunsawian species, was a secret experiment of combined Rat, Fox, and Human DNA, via unethical practice. Despite their human-like behaviour, specimens were treated like animals. Held in labs, the first expies were experimented on, and had their genes cloned. Humans are intentionally left vague on purpose, as to leave it up for interpretation. However, it is stated that the companies involved with creating Gunsawians would "rather nuke the entire company and erase all evidence of the bioengineering stuff than let it get out to the public"
Due to this, the lifespan of an experiment is short, fizzling out at the max age of 40.
You play as a non-lab creation in a non-distant future, created via reproduction from other experiment. You are trained and will eventually be sent to the Grey Planet between the age of 10-28. It is stated that the majority of Gunsawians are sent to the Grey Planet, and only a select few can live out their lives on Earth.
Upon being sent into the Grey Planet, most experiments are [[Unchipped mode|chipped]]. * This chip allows the experiment to see their health by focusing, as well as radiation levels. However, this also acts as a kill switch, allowing death through [[Radiation_line|specialized radiation]].
=== The Grey Planet ===
The setting of the game is described as a Grey Planet flourishing with life. The game explores the many diverse [[layers]] of this planet.
There are zero humans on the Grey Planet during any of the three games, a time period of 95 years since Causalities: Unknown. Given the existence of a very easy space travel path to the Grey Planet, Humanity has decided to send any excess junk to the planet. Because of this, the mountains of trash and scrap piles, alongside accidental sends of medical equipment, containers, and everything are found in the current game. Due to a prior world war on earth, old guns, specialized booby traps and dangers are sent to the Grey Planet to be disposed of, thus forming the traps found in the game.
== Ending == {{Hidden | header = Endings (Spoilers) | content =
=== Endings: === - '''Normal:''' Die within the 12 layers. Nothing special will occur. A death screen and stats will show. - '''Cargo Surface Death:''' Staying on the surface for too long will blow up the cargo. There will be a special death screen of the character gored and the mission status will display 'CRITICAL FAILURE'. Upon reaching the surface in layer 12, the game is complete, with a potential optional lab escape sequence, if the player has a gun. Any following ending will unlock an additional character if the player does not have all playable characters already: - '''Test Tube Ending:''' Failing to bring a firearm or choosing the option to not use it will end with the player character ending in the test tube indefinitely for studying. - '''Chipped Lab Escape Ending''': When the character is chipped, starting the lab escape will have the player surgically removing the chip themselves, thus taking major brain damage. - '''Unchipped Escape Ending''': Escaping the lab without brain damage when unchipped will lead to a good ending. - '''Dune Ending''': Escaping the lab as Dune, unchipped, and without brain damage will lead to a special ending... - '''Gunsaw Prelude / Mercenary Ending:''' Finding three exceptionally rare chip (levitation, reformation, mindswap) alongside a scarf, whilst ignoring the escape pod for a normal ending will trigger the Mercenary Ending, which leads into the events of [[Gunsaw]].
}} == Future ==
The canon ending of Casualties: Unknown has the battery explode, thus breaking. Another game by Orsoniks, [[Gunsaw]], is set 10-15 [?] years after Casualties: Unknown. Through natural death, radiation, and potentially another unknown cause, the majority of beings in CU do not live to see the Gunsaw world. The [[Gunsaw#Characters|Mercenary]] is one of the few, due to their body-swapping tech.
In Gunsaw, several different species originating from the [[Experiment]] are present, as well as basic civilization and warfare. These new species were sent by the Company, as they remove every Gunsawian from their labs. During the events of Gooey 78-85 years after CU, there are no more Gunsawians on Earth. Alongside the new species, the Company sent a incredibly large number of firearms onto the Grey Planet to observe what would happen. This naturally led to war, thus forming the setting for Gunsaw. On the Grey Planet, however, exist 100-200 thousands of Gunsawians.
In the far future, 70 years after the events of Gunsaw, the same lab company that has created Gunsawians sends Goo monsters for testing to another planet, this one without a breathable atmosphere. During this time, there exists tens to hundreds of millions of Gunsawians on the Grey Planet. As Earth no longer sends junk and supplies to the Grey Planet and instead dumps it on the alternative planet, the Gunsawian population stabilizes. During this era, the events of another of Orson's games, Gooey, begins. In Gooey, you play as a volunteer working for The Company. You control a Goo monster's brain via a specialized chip, and fight off other Goo monsters on this new planet. It has also been confirmed that goo monsters have been sent to the Grey Planet and interacted with Gunsawians, where Gunsawians have experimented with taking goo monsters down the planet's [[layers]] out of curiosity.

It's worth noting that the coats merely TRY to do things, and while sometimes they try to help, those attempts are usually far less successful than the ones where they try to mess you up.

The next day, they came back with ELEVEN arms.
The humans removed ten of those, only to grace them with seven new ones.
The next day it was five.
Today it's four.

It's quiet. Those who get lucky often just rest; sometimes they socialize. Usually, they just worry about those who got taken.

I don't understand it. Why is each sleep cycle divided into 24 (?) hours? What governs such rules? Even the humans play by it.
Is something above them controlling it?


Some more hours later, the first test subjects start to trickle back in.
Best-case scenario, they walk in on their own. Quite common, actually.
Sometimes they are carried in.
Rarely, two guards come in instead. They head straight to someone's cot and take away their things.
If it were your friend, and the guards are some of the nicer ones, they will let you keep a thing or two.
I don't really have anyone to wait for anymore, so I just watch those who do. Everyone is used to this, but the nerves are always present.


Dinner time.
The meal dispensers dropped some cooked meat on my plate. Lucky. Better than breakfast, for sure.
Some others hum approvingly, as they get similarly lucky. Others grumble as they get served the same old nutrient bars. It's really like they are feeding us with whatever they have on hand... Maybe this is a test, too?
Sometimes fights break out over the food, but that's rare. The guards are not happy anytime this happens.

People talk. They play. They sing.
Some nurse their wounds, or help others nurse theirs.
It's a calm time.
The coats are done for the time being.

Later.
In about an \"hour\", our last meal of the day will be served.
Bedtime soon after that.
There is not much more to say, really.

Actually, there is one thing. Very rarely, extra tests will be called around this time. Well, the humans call them \"Relocations\" or \"Assignments.\"

You never come back when you get called to one of those. But they always allow you to take your things. I am not sure if it's because they are really taking you somewhere else, or they simply want to expedite the process of emptying your place by having you do it yourself.

: Yes. That's nine prizes. If we all agreed to donate the cash from those to [], we'd end up with roughly fifteen million dollars. A drop in a bucket compared to the financial black hole that is your idiotic project!

[ rises from his chair.]

: IDIOTIC?! IDIOTIC?! WE ARE CREATING LIFE! You are a witness to the infancy of a new intelligent species! Our colleagues would ''kill'' for such an opportunity mere ten years ago!

: Kill? Sure. Pay what you ask? Hell. No.

: Is it always money with you? Fine! We are making money! Even our plushie line is more than enough to cover-

: Pay more than cash, you cretin! The moment this whole affair ends up on some nosy journalist's desk, we will all pay with our ''lives.'' Hell, our families will be lynched. Besides, the plushies do not require us to keep your colony of… talking rats alive.

: The Sawians are not talking rats! They are a mix of-

: OH, EXCUUUSE ME! I meant to say walking tickets to a dematerializer! OUR TICKETS! I am not ending up before the ICC, just because you are a greedy little fu-

: Gentlemen. Please.

: I- Ah. Apologies .

: Doctor . Please make your case. Why are those creatures worth keeping?

[Silence. sits down.]

: I mean... There are so many reasons to pick, I-

: Then I trust that you will be able to pick the right one.

: Uh. Right. Okay. Braingrow! We have Braingrow!

: Yes, the commercially available medicine that one can purchase at the corner store.

: No. Not this one. I could bash someone's head straight open. Introduce the frontal lobe to a hammer. Corner store Braingrow won't help here. Ours will have the fellow walking and talking within a WEEK. Without such an ample supply of human-like test subjects, we'd need decades to perfect the nanomachines' operational directives. Twice that for testing.

: Of course. . Your counterpoint?

: Yeah. He's got results. The moment the public finds out just HOW he got them, we are all done for. What are you going to say, [#######]? That the creatures whose likeness we sell to kids exist, and that you enjoy cracking their heads in your spare time? There will be no shifting the blame. We will answer for this; the entire Company will. They'll even lynch our goddamned janitor!

[The nods, watching the two silently for a moment.]

: I now understand why you clash with such intensity. It is not in our nature to back down when the truth is on our side. And the truth is… You are both correct.

[Silence.]

: The Sawians pose a risk that we cannot afford to take. They are also an opportunity that we cannot afford to let go. We have them in our grasp. And we must make sure things remain that way.

: But… how, ?

-Sawians confined to their cells until further notice.
-Employees are forbidden from leaving the grounds until further notice.
-Employee contracts extended indefinitely.
-Carrying firearms made mandatory for Security personnel
-Facility-wide thermal remediation procedures approved and pre-authorized. == Trivia == '' The world shares similar characteristics with the world of [[Gunsaw]], another game made by the same developer. :''Not to be mistaken with [[Tile fluids]]'' ------ Fluids are substances that can be put in [[Fluid Containers|fluid containers]]. Dropping a [[Fluid Containers|fluid container]] in a puddle of any [[Tile Fluids|Tile Fluid]] will fill it with the fluid, 200ml of fluid per block of tile fluid. Fluids can be drank by dragging most fluid containers to the middle of your inventory, same as with eating food, applied (by dragging a fluid container containing the fluid to a limb on the health menu) and injected, by using a handful of special fluid containers that have that ability. (Drag the fluid container to the injection site on the health menu, same as with applying. Some syringes will start the syringe minigame, while others inject a certain predetermined amount.)
